codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex challenged 9 of 14 rows. Key themes: (1) process_doc is too weak when enforcement is needed — push to harness_fix; (2) unshippable artifacts (gitignored / out-of-repo) are routinely under-rated — should be critical; (3) destructive first-runs and resource-pressure scenarios need real sandboxed fixtures not dry-run/PATH-shim mocks; (4) R5's 4 sub-items must be split for accurate ownership/severity; (5) resume/quota/score share a common pattern of missing event-sourced lifecycle logging.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Adopted codex challenges on W2, W4, R2, R3, R4, R6, R8, R9. Split R5 into R5a-R5d. Upgraded R3 and R2 to critical. Strengthened concrete_fix sentences across the table.

rows.summary: Orchestrator set spec_path=null despite latest spec file existing on disk
rows.concrete_fix: Add a pre-dispatch spec resolver in /dev that fails or asks before spec_path=null whenever an eligible latest spec exists, logging an explicit override reason when null is intentional.

rows.summary: test-writer skipped despite STANDARD tier (bash hooks don't fit pytest skeleton)
rows.concrete_fix: Add a dispatch gate requiring language-appropriate test-writer coverage for STANDARD changes — Bash hooks/scripts route to shellcheck+Bats+fixture tests or a recorded waiver — instead of silently skipping.
rows.codex_note: Promoted from process_doc to harness_fix; mandatory rules need enforcement, not prose.

rows.summary: Two quota cuts mid-cycle (BA iter1, QA final-verify) required silent resumption
rows.concrete_fix: Instrument agent dispatch/resumption with visible quota-wall events, pending-task preservation, and a warning/block threshold before BA/QA-critical phases.
rows.codex_note: Tightened from generic telemetry to actionable thresholds + task preservation.

rows.summary: Completion report had to be written via subagent because Write gate was already consumed
rows.concrete_fix: Add a final-report preflight that reports consumed/non-consumed gated tools and either reserves the Write gate for the report or explicitly routes report generation through a subagent before the gate is exhausted.
rows.codex_note: Reclassified from mixed → systemic; gate-consumption visibility is a recurring orchestrator problem.

rows.summary: No quota-wall escalation to user — silent agent resumption may have degraded reasoning
rows.concrete_fix: Make every quota-wall/resumption emit a user-visible checkpoint showing what stopped, what context was retained/dropped, and what tasks remain before work continues.

rows.summary: docs/reference/tmp-cleanup-convention.md in .gitignore (docs/ blanket); AC8 passed on-disk but artifact won't ship
rows.concrete_fix: QA Step 7 must compare required deliverables against git status, gitignore matches, and pending-commit contents, failing critical if any required artifact is ignored or unshippable.

rows.summary: /usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh is out-of-repo root-owned; single-host only, no install recipe
rows.concrete_fix: Add a release gate requiring an out-of-repo manifest plus idempotent setup/install recipe for every system-path modification, or an explicit host-local waiver.
rows.codex_note: Upgraded major→critical; out-of-repo system changes without reproducible install are an irreversibility risk.

rows.summary: All AC4/AC5 verification used --dry-run; first real rm runs tonight at cron 02:07
rows.concrete_fix: Require sandboxed real-effect deletion tests on synthetic files before enabling any destructive scheduled job, plus same-day post-cron verification for this shipped change.
rows.codex_note: Upgraded mixed/major → systemic/critical; destructive scheduled jobs that have never been exercised live are a recurring risk pattern.

rows.summary: AC2 WARN-marker verified via PATH-shim mock df, not real >75% disk pressure
rows.concrete_fix: Build a bounded tmpfs/disk-pressure integration fixture and require threshold-behavior tests there; keep PATH shims only for unit-level isolation.
rows.codex_note: Reclassified mixed/minor → systemic/major; reusable harness gap, not a one-cycle shortcut.

rows.summary: Counter file on /tmp under ENOSPC — rate-limiter fails open if /tmp is full
rows.concrete_fix: Move counter persistence off /tmp (e.g., /var/lib/claude/) and add ENOSPC-safe fallback that fails closed when persistence is impossible.

rows.summary: Long-session non-saturated counter cleanup → rate-limit reset semantics drift
rows.concrete_fix: Add a TTL-based counter sweep with explicit semantics for non-saturated sessions, recording reset events alongside saturation events.
Bump settings.json timeout to 20s or trim L1.5 budget to ≤12s; spec-alignment item, single-config change.

rows.summary: -mtime +N doc precision (>3d documented but actually means 72-96h)
rows.concrete_fix: Update tmp-cleanup-convention.md to document find -mtime +N semantics as 'older than N*24h, granularity 24h' and add a test that asserts the boundary.

rows.summary: Orphan commit 34210cc bundles 68 unrelated files across multiple subsystems
rows.concrete_fix: Add a /dev preflight that blocks or explicitly baselines dirty/orphaned pre-cycle state and records the baseline ref in the final report so attribution is preserved.
rows.codex_note: Reclassified one-off/accept → systemic/major; a dirty baseline blocks per-cycle attribution and pollutes origin history.

rows.summary: Upload token written with session_id='unknown' (CLAUDE_SESSION_ID not exported)
rows.concrete_fix: Initialize and export CLAUDE_SESSION_ID once at orchestrator-shell startup and fail uploads (rather than silently writing 'unknown') if the env var is missing.

rows.summary: codex_required=false to BA iter1 resumption overridden by Stop-hook; scope extended by 10 new findings
rows.concrete_fix: Make explicit task flags such as codex_required=false override resume/Stop-hook defaults unless a hard-blocking hook emits a visible reason and asks for user confirmation.
rows.codex_note: Strengthened from 'document precedence' (too weak) to deterministic flag-honoring enforcement.

rows.summary: Score variance: dev 81 after qa_first_pass, close_success started from 73 (8-point gap, no event)
rows.concrete_fix: Replace direct score writes with an append-only transition log plus atomic compare-and-swap update that records prev_score, new_score, actor, reason, and fails when the prev value doesn't match.
rows.codex_note: Strengthened from 'investigate atomicity' (hand-wavy) to event-sourced transitions with CAS.

top_three_systemic_fixes.rationale: R1 (gitignored doc), R2 (out-of-repo root-owned script), and R5a (counter on ephemeral /tmp) all ship apparent success while leaving the deliverable non-reproducible. A single QA Step 7 gate that diffs files_modified against gitignore + checks for out-of-repo paths + flags ephemeral persistence catches all three with one harness change. High impact, high tractability.

top_three_systemic_fixes.theme: Destructive / resource-pressure verification with real fixtures (not dry-run/PATH-shim)
top_three_systemic_fixes.rationale: R3 ships a destructive cron job that has never been exercised live; R4 validates threshold logic against a mocked df. Building a bounded sandbox fixture (tmpfs container with synthetic files + size limits) addresses both with one reusable harness, and prevents a recurring class of false-positive PASS verdicts where dry-run/mock substitutes for real behavior.

top_three_systemic_fixes.theme: Event-sourced visible lifecycle (quota walls, agent resumption, score transitions)
top_three_systemic_fixes.rationale: These three share a single root pattern: silent state changes the user can't see and the system can't audit. A unified lifecycle-event log (append-only, with actor/reason/from/to fields, surfaced to user on quota walls and resumption) addresses quota-cut visibility (W3), silent resumption (W5), and score gaps (R9) at once. High tractability because all three sites already exist in scripts/.

self_verification.notes: All 14 rows have classification, severity, and remediation_tier. All harness_fix and process_doc rows have one-sentence concrete_fix. R5 split into 4 sub-rows per codex. No rows were skipped or marked incomplete.

scope_note: This is a meta-assessment artifact, not a CLOSE verdict. No verdict line emitted.
